latestPlannerResponse.message: Here is the plan for Empty Java Backend Service Setup.
latestPlannerResponse.featureUnderstanding: Set up an empty Java 21 Spring Boot 3.x Maven project for the Rivvy Portal application in the RS Service Tier, representing Rivvy Portal Services. Use Maven coordinates com.rivvystudios:portal:0.0.1 and main package com.rivvystudios.portal. Include baseline starters: web, actuator, validation; provide standard source/test/resources structure, an application entrypoint, and minimal configuration with dev/test/prod profiles. Expose only Actuator health (no business endpoints), and include a minimal README. No containerization, CI, security, persistence, or OpenAPI at this stage.

in: Single-module Spring Boot 3.x Maven project for Rivvy Portal Services within the Rivvy Portal application (RS Service Tier)
Main package: com.rivvystudios.portal and an application entrypoint class
Standard src/main/java, src/main/resources, src/test/java folder structure
Application configuration with dev, test, prod profiles, setting spring.application.name and server.port
Expose only Actuator health (and default info not exposed over web)
out: Business/domain controllers or any Portal API endpoints
Database, messaging, cache, or external integrations
Advanced observability (custom logging formats, tracing, metrics beyond defaults)
Code quality tooling (Checkstyle/Spotless) and editor configs

assumptions: Packaging is an executable JAR using the Spring Boot parent/BOM.
Application profiles are named dev, test, and prod.
spring.application.name will be rivvy-portal-services across profiles.
Default server.port is 8080 for all profiles.
Only Actuator health is exposed over web by default (no additional exposure configured).
No controllers or REST endpoints are created now, despite including the web starter.
Default Logback configuration is used; no JSON logging.
JUnit 5 is used via spring-boot-starter-test with a context-loads and a health check smoke test.
No integration with the Portal API interface at this time; that will come later.
acceptanceCriteria: Project builds and tests pass with Maven on Java 21 using Spring Boot 3.x parent/BOM.
Main class in package com.rivvystudios.portal starts the application successfully.
Standard src/main/java, src/main/resources, and src/test/java directories exist.
Dependencies include web, actuator, validation, and test; no additional starters are present.
Application profiles dev, test, and prod exist with spring.application.name=rivvy-portal-services.
Server starts on port 8080 by default.
No custom REST controllers exist; only Actuator endpoints are available.
GET /actuator/health returns a 200 status when the app is running.
README describes how to build and run the service for each profile.

implementationPlan.planTitle: Implementation Plan for Empty Java Backend Service Setup

increments.title: Scaffold Rivvy Portal Services Spring Boot project (Java 21, Maven) with health-only Actuator
increments.intent:
- Repository and module setup
  - Create a single-module Maven project named portal aligned to Rivvy Portal Services (RS Service Tier) for the Rivvy Portal application.
  - Establish standard directories: src/main/java, src/main/resources, src/test/java.
  - Use main package com.rivvystudios.portal; add placeholder subpackages (empty package-info.java files or .gitkeep) for future layers: config, controller, service, domain, repository (no classes yet).
  - Add a root-level .gitignore for Maven/Java/IDE artifacts (target/, .idea/, .project, .classpath, .settings/, *.iml, .DS_Store).

- Maven configuration (pom.xml)
  - groupId=com.rivvystudios, artifactId=portal, version=0.0.1, packaging=jar, name=Rivvy Portal Services.
  - Parent: org.springframework.boot:spring-boot-starter-parent with a pinned 3.x version (use latest stable 3.3.x available in your environment).
  - Properties: java.version=21; project.build.sourceEncoding=UTF-8; maven.compiler.release=21.
  - Dependencies:
    - org.springframework.boot:spring-boot-starter-web
    - org.springframework.boot:spring-boot-starter-actuator
    - org.springframework.boot:spring-boot-starter-validation
    - test scope: org.springframework.boot:spring-boot-starter-test
  - Build plugins:
    - org.springframework.boot:spring-boot-maven-plugin (no special configuration required; optional mainClass=“com.rivvystudios.portal.PortalApplication”).
  - Do not include persistence, security, OpenAPI, or messaging starters.

- Application entrypoint
  - Create com.rivvystudios.portal.PortalApplication with @SpringBootApplication and main(String[] args) that runs SpringApplication.

- Configuration and profiles (YAML)
  - src/main/resources/application.yml (common):
    - spring.application.name: rivvy-portal-services
    - server.port: 8080
    - management.endpoints.web.exposure.include: health (explicit to ensure only health is exposed)
    - management.endpoint.health.probes.enabled: true (enables liveness/readiness if needed later; still exposed under /actuator/health)
  - src/main/resources/application-dev.yml: inherit defaults; optionally set logging.level.root: INFO.
  - src/main/resources/application-test.yml: inherit defaults; optionally set logging.level.root: WARN to reduce noise during tests.
  - src/main/resources/application-prod.yml: inherit defaults; optionally set logging.level.root: INFO.
  - Do not set spring.profiles.active in files; allow activation via command line or environment.

- Testing
  - Create a context load test: com.rivvystudios.portal.PortalApplicationTests with @SpringBootTest to validate the application context starts.
  - Add a health endpoint smoke test:
    - @SpringBootTest(webEnvironment = SpringBootTest.WebEnvironment.RANDOM_PORT)
    - Inject org.springframework.boot.test.web.client.TestRestTemplate and assert GET /actuator/health returns 200 and status="UP".

- Documentation
  - Add README.md with:
    - Project purpose and relation to Rivvy Portal Services (RS Service Tier).
    - Prerequisites: Java 21, Maven 3.9+.
    - Build: mvn clean verify
    - Run: mvn spring-boot:run or java -jar target/portal-0.0.1.jar
    - Profiles: --spring.profiles.active=dev|test|prod
    - Health check: curl http://localhost:8080/actuator/health
    - Notes: no business endpoints, future API to be added via Portal API.

- Validation and sign-off
  - Ensure mvn clean verify passes locally on Java 21.
  - Start the app with each profile (dev/test/prod) to confirm successful boot and /actuator/health returns 200.
  - Confirm no controllers exist and no non-Actuator endpoints respond.
  - Tag initial commit/release as v0.0.1 if versioning workflow requires.

messages.content: Hi! Ready to support the “Empty Java Backend Service Setup” feature, and I have the product backlog and architecture context on hand. Are there any specific architecture entities or diagrams you’d like to highlight as most relevant for this work item?
I've provided the architectural context, please give me your understanding of this feature
Here’s my current understanding of the empty Java backend setup for Rivvy Portal; I have 7 questions to confirm scope and dependencies.

Answers to your questions:

Q: Confirm Maven coordinates: groupId=com.rivvystudios, artifactId=portal, version=0.0.1?
A: confirmed

Q: Single-module or multi-module structure (e.g., core, api) for Rivvy Portal Services?
A: single

Q: Which initial dependencies should be included (e.g., web, actuator, validation, lombok, springdoc-openapi, testcontainers)?
A: web, actuator, validation

Q: Include containerization (Dockerfile/Jib) and a basic CI workflow now, or defer?
A: defer

Q: Create environment profiles (dev/test/prod) and set spring.application.name and default server.port? If so, what values?
A: yes, you decide names

Q: Scaffold only health via Actuator, or also a base versioned API path (e.g., /api/v1) with an empty controller?
A: Scaffold only health via Actuator

Q: Any code quality and formatting standards to enforce (Checkstyle/Spotless, .editorconfig), and preferred package structure conventions?
A: none
Thanks—requirements confirmed. I’m ready to produce the spec for the empty Java backend setup.
Okay, let's see if we need to break this work item into parts...
This item is small enough, so let's get the software architect to review this.
